discovery.run_summary: Slate is dominated by SaaS names where every earnings 8-K fell back to the cover document — no Exhibit 99.1 numbers or guidance language were readable, sharply limiting filing-evidence quality. The two cleanest reads are GTLB (pre-announced Q1 in-line with guidance plus an RIF — derisked) and VSCO (10-K disclosed a tariff hit and management is mitigating into a holiday-comp-quarter print). MDB and ASAN have material CEO/COO turnover noise in the bundle but no financials to anchor a direction; ESTC has nothing readable beyond the 10-K.

discoveries.name: GitLab Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: May 11, 2026 8-K explicitly states GitLab 'expects its results for the first quarter of fiscal year 2027 to be in-line with its guidance' and reaffirms FY27 guidance issued March 3, 2026. The same 8-K announces a reduction in force intended to 'realign operating structure' with the 'majority of the resulting savings' reinvested in strategic growth initiatives — a margin-positive setup three weeks ahead of print. The 10-K (filed 2026-03-17) shows FY26 revenue $955.2M, +26% growth, DBNRR 118%, $100K+ customers up to 1,456 from 1,229, and operating cash flow margin swinging from (8)% to 24% — all directionally supportive.
discoveries.guidance_pattern: Management has just publicly reaffirmed both Q1 and FY27 guidance within three weeks of the print, which is unusually committal and historically tends to precede a clean in-line-to-better result.
discoveries.setup: Pre-announced in-line Q1 plus cost-out RIF and reaffirmed FY guide, 6 trading days before the print
discoveries.thesis: Filings show a derisked print: explicit T-15 reaffirmation removes downside surprise risk on the top line, while the May 11 RIF is a fresh, filings-grounded margin tailwind the Street may not yet have fully modeled. With the 10-K already showing FCF margin expansion and DBNRR holding at 118%, the combination of confirmed revenue and newly announced cost takeout skews the print toward better-than-expected operating leverage.
discoveries.what_confirms: Q1 revenue/RPO land at or above the reaffirmed range AND FY27 operating margin guide is raised to reflect RIF savings; net new $100K+ customers continue tracking >25% YoY.
discoveries.what_kills: Management uses the RIF as cover to cut FY27 revenue guidance (signaling demand weakness behind the cost action), or DBNRR steps down meaningfully from 118%, or AI/Duo monetization commentary disappoints despite reaffirmation.
discoveries.what_to_learn: A T-15 reaffirmation 8-K combined with a same-filing RIF is a high-information event — the company is essentially pre-clearing the print while structurally improving margin; track whether these setups systematically lead to positive earnings reactions.
research_pointers: Compare May 11 reaffirmed FY27 guide line-by-line to the March 3 original to confirm no soft tells
Quantify RIF size and expected annualized savings vs. consensus FY27 opex
Check whether the new CFO (Jessica Ross, started ~Jan 15, 2026) is likely to set a conservative tone on her first guide
Monitor Duo Agent Platform GA (January 2026) early monetization commentary

discoveries.name: Victoria's Secret & Co.
discoveries.filings_evidence: FY2025 10-K (filed 2026-03-20) discloses tariffs 'negatively impacted operating income during fiscal 2025 by approximately $85 million' net of mitigation and explicitly flags continuing uncertainty including a Feb 20, 2026 Supreme Court ruling that struck down IEEPA tariffs but a new 10% Section 122 global tariff that replaced them. Risk Factors call out that 'many of our mitigation strategies require significant lead time' and pricing power is 'limited by competitive dynamics and consumer price sensitivity.' Q3 10-Q (filed 2025-12-05) states 'no material changes' to 10-K risk factors. The 10-K also discloses recent senior leadership turnover (new CEO Sept 2024, new CFO/COO Aug 2025, new CMO Aug 2025) and store count declining from 882 to 858.
discoveries.guidance_pattern: Management issued updated full-year guidance with both Q2 (June 11, 2025) and Q3 (Dec 5, 2025) prints — a pattern of intra-year revisions consistent with a tariff/cost backdrop they are still calibrating.
discoveries.setup: Q1 print with fresh tariff-cost overhang into a soft seasonal quarter and a still-new leadership team
discoveries.thesis: Filings show a quantified, ongoing cost headwind ($85M FY25 op income hit from tariffs) layered on top of a still-shrinking store fleet and a leadership team that turned over its CEO, CFO/COO, and CMO within ~12 months. Q1 is structurally the weakest quarter for an intimates retailer and is the first one fully exposed to the new Section 122 10% global tariff layered over a leadership team that just took the seat. The combination raises the probability of either a Q1 miss or — more likely — a cautious FY26 guide that the market is under-pricing.
discoveries.what_confirms: Q1 gross margin compresses YoY beyond what the prior guide implied; FY26 EPS guide is cut and management cites tariffs as the primary driver; inventory growth outpaces sales growth.
discoveries.what_kills: Mitigation actions (pricing, sourcing shifts) already in place show up as in-line gross margin; management quantifies a smaller-than-expected FY26 tariff impact; Q1 comps positive on a clean basis.
discoveries.what_to_learn: When a 10-K quantifies a specific dollar tariff hit AND flags that mitigation 'requires significant lead time,' the first quarter immediately following is the period where the unmitigated cost shows up most cleanly — track whether T+1 reactions in such names are systematically negative.
research_pointers: Quantify the Q1 portion of the $85M FY25 tariff hit and how the new Section 122 10% global tariff layers on top
Check whether the March 5, 2026 Q4 release issued FY26 guidance and what tariff assumption it embeds
Watch for inventory commentary — the 10-K flags pre-holiday inventory builds as a margin-risk lever
New CFO/COO Scott Sekella's first full guidance cycle: tone of conservatism on first FY26 print

skipped.reason: All four earnings 8-Ks fell back to the cover document — no Exhibit 99.1 financial or guidance language to anchor a direction; 10-Q risk factors state no material change. Filing evidence too thin — UNCLEAR.

All four earnings 8-Ks fell back to cover documents; the Nov 3, 2025 CEO change (Ittycheria out, Desai in) and Feb 27, 2026 President Field Operations resignation are material disruptions but cut both ways with no Exhibit 99.1 to anchor — UNCLEAR.

All four earnings 8-Ks fell back to cover documents; COO and GC both resigned Nov 30, 2025 and 10-K discloses ongoing net losses but no financial trajectory readable from the 8-Ks. Filing evidence too mixed — UNCLEAR.
